concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains a meaningful agency logo (Seal of the North Carolina Marine Fisheries) on the top left of the first page. According to the image manifest, the extracted image data for this logo has a `b64_len` of 0, meaning it is corrupt/invalid. In the HTML output, it appears merely as a text placeholder `[Image: Seal of the North Carolina Marine Fisheries celebrating 200 years of management and conservation (1822-2022)]` rather than a valid `` element. The primary agency seal on t
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Top left of page 1 shows the circular seal of the North Carolina Marine Fisheries celebrating 200 years.
concerns.output_quote: [Image: Seal of the North Carolina Marine Fisheries celebrating 200 years of management and conservation (1822-2022)]

concerns.analysis: The main structured list in the document uses Roman numerals (I. SIZE LIMIT, II. HARVEST PERIOD AND LIMITS, III. GEAR RESTRICTIONS, IV. GENERAL INFORMATION). In the output for page 1, items I, II, and III are correctly extracted as items within a single ordered list. However, on page 2, 'IV. GENERAL INFORMATION' is extracted as a level 2 heading (`h2`), and its sub-items (A-J) are extracted as a completely new top-level ordered list instead of being nested children. This incorrectly splits the c
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
IV. GENERAL INFORMATION
A. This proclamation is issued...

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 3 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=3, examples=['https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-50/part-648/subpart-i', 'https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-50/part-648/subpart-i', 'http://www.ncmarinefisheries.net/']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: 2 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-50/part-; www.ncmarinefisheries.net.
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=2. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links. Per NCDIT criteria, link text not wrapped in href is a major defect.

The source document relies heavily on underlining to indicate visual emphasis (e.g., all section headings like 'I. SIZE LIMIT', 'II. HARVEST PERIOD AND LIMITS', and entire list items such as II. A-E and III. M). The extraction output completely loses this underline emphasis, returning plain text.
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
II. HARVEST PERIOD AND LIMITS
A. It is unlawful for persons participating in a commercial fishing operation employing fish pots, traps, and/or hook and line gear, regardless of the number of people involved, to possess or land more than 2,000 pounds of black sea bass per trip. (entire text is underlined)
concerns.output_quote: II. HARVEST PERIOD AND LIMITS... A. It is unlawful for persons participating in a commercial fishing operation employing fish pots, traps, and/or hook and line gear, regardless of the number of people involved, to possess or land more than 2,000 pounds of black sea bass per trip. (no underline emphasis)

concerns.description:
Header/footer elements retained in output (1): State of North Carolina | Division of Marine Fisheries

concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.
